sourceQuestion:
25 persons are in a room. 15 of them play hockey, 17 of them play football and 10 of them play both hockey and football. Then the number of persons playing neither hockey nor football is:

A. 2
B. 17
C. 13
D. 3

parsed.stem: 25 persons are in a room. 15 of them play hockey, 17 of them play football and 10 of them play both hockey and football. Then the number of persons playing neither hockey nor football is:

sourceQuestion:
Among 150 faculty members in an institute, 55 are connected with each other through Facebook and 65 are connected through Whatsapp. 30 faculty members do not have Facebook or Whatsapp accounts. The number of faculty members connected only through Facebook accounts is:

A. 35
B. 45
C. 65
D. 90

parsed.stem: Among 150 faculty members in an institute, 55 are connected with each other through Facebook and 65 are connected through Whatsapp. 30 faculty members do not have Facebook or Whatsapp accounts. The number of faculty members connected only through Facebook accounts is:

sourceQuestion:
In the given diagram, teachers are represented in the triangle, researchers in the circle and administrators in the rectangle. Out of the total number of people, the percentage of administrators shall be in the range of _______

(The triangle has vertices showing numbers; the circle and rectangle overlap with the triangle. From the figure: triangle region = 50, circle region = 40, rectangle region overlapping areas. The figure shows: 25 inside triangle only, 10 inside circle only, 15 inside rectangle only, 5 in triangle+circle, 8 in circle+rectangle, 12 in triangle+rectangle, 2 in all three. Total = 77.)

A. 0 to 15
B. 16 to 30
C. 31 to 45
D. 46 to 60

The figure shows: 25 inside triangle only, 10 inside circle only, 15 inside rectangle only, 5 in triangle+circle, 8 in circle+rectangle, 12 in triangle+rectangle, 2 in all three. Total = 77.)

sourceQuestion:
In a college, there are three student clubs. Students are only in the Drama club are 40; students only in the Dance club are 30; students only in the Maths club are 12; students in both Drama and Dance clubs are 12; students in both Dance and Maths clubs are 7; students in both Drama and Maths clubs are 2; and students in all clubs are 2. If 75% of the students in the college are members of at least one of these clubs, then the total number of students in the college is:

A. 1000
B. 975
C. 900
D. 1,500

parsed.stem: In a college, there are three student clubs. Students are only in the Drama club are 40; students only in the Dance club are 30; students only in the Maths club are 12; students in both Drama and Dance clubs are 12; students in both Dance and Maths clubs are 7; students in both Drama and Maths clubs are 2; and students in all clubs are 2. If 75% of the students in the college are members of at least one of these clubs, then the total number of students in the college is:

sourceQuestion:

A. 200  B. 150  C. 100  D. 50

parsed.stem: In a school of 100 students, 600 play chess and 300 play football. If 50 students play both chess and football, the number of students who play neither is ________.

sourceQuestion:
In a company, 30% of the employees drink coffee, 40% of the employees drink tea and 10% of the employees drink both tea and coffee. What % of employees drink neither tea nor coffee?

A. 15  B. 25  C. 40  D. 49

parsed.stem: In a company, 30% of the employees drink coffee, 40% of the employees drink tea and 10% of the employees drink both tea and coffee. What % of employees drink neither tea nor coffee?

sourceQuestion:

- 60 students are interested in Mathematics and Physics.

- 20 students are interested in Mathematics and Chemistry.
- 30 students are interested in Mathematics, Physics, and Chemistry.

- 20 students are interested in Mathematics and Chemistry.
- 30 students are interested in Mathematics, Physics, and Chemistry.
- Remaining students are interested in Humanities.

sourceQuestion:
In a class of 100 students,
i. there are 30 students who neither like romantic movies nor comedy movies,
ii. the number of students who like romantic movies is twice the number of students who like comedy movies, and
iii. the number of students who like both romantic movies and comedy movies is 20.
How many students in the class like romantic movies?

A. 40  B. 20  C. 60  D. 30

parsed.stem:
In a class of 100 students,
i. there are 30 students who neither like romantic movies nor comedy movies,
ii. the number of students who like romantic movies is twice the number of students who like comedy movies, and
iii. the number of students who like both romantic movies and comedy movies is 20.
How many students in the class like romantic movies?

sourceQuestion:
A gathering of 50 linguists discovered that 4 knew Kannada, Telugu and Tamil; 7 knew only Telugu and Tamil; 5 knew only Kannada and Tamil; and 24 knew only Telugu and Kannada. If the number of linguists who knew Tamil is 21 and those who knew Kannada is also 24, how many linguists knew only Telugu?

A. 9  B. 10  C. 11  D. 8

parsed.stem: A gathering of 50 linguists discovered that 4 knew Kannada, Telugu and Tamil; 7 knew only Telugu and Tamil; 5 knew only Kannada and Tamil; and 24 knew only Telugu and Kannada. If the number of linguists who knew Tamil is 21 and those who knew Kannada is also 24, how many linguists knew only Telugu?

sourceQuestion:
In a class of 200 students in an M.Tech programme, each student is required to take at least one subject from the following three:
  M600: Advanced Engineering Mathematics
  C600: Computational Methods for Engineers
  E600: Experimental Techniques for Engineers

The registration data for the M.Tech class shows that 100 students have taken M600, 200 students have taken C600, and 40 students have taken E600. The maximum possible number of students who have taken all three subjects is:

A. 20  B. 30  C. 40  D. 50

parsed.stem:
In a class of 200 students in an M.Tech programme, each student is required to take at least one subject from the following three:
  M600: Advanced Engineering Mathematics
  C600: Computational Methods for Engineers
  E600: Experimental Techniques for Engineers

The registration data for the M.Tech class shows that 100 students have taken M600, 200 students have taken C600, and 40 students have taken E600. The maximum possible number of students who have taken all three subjects is:

sourceQuestion:
There are 36 teachers in a school. There are 11 who can teach Thermodynamics (TD), 5 who can teach Electrical Sciences (ES), and 5 who can teach Engineering Mechanics (EM). There are a total of 36 teachers. 4 cannot teach any of the three subjects. 6 can teach only ES. 4 can teach all three subjects, i.e., TD, ES and TD. 4 can teach TD and ES but NOT EM. How many teachers can teach only TD?

A. 0  B. 2  C. 3  D. 4

parsed.stem: There are 36 teachers in a school. There are 11 who can teach Thermodynamics (TD), 5 who can teach Electrical Sciences (ES), and 5 who can teach Engineering Mechanics (EM). There are a total of 36 teachers. 4 cannot teach any of the three subjects. 6 can teach only ES. 4 can teach all three subjects, i.e., TD, ES and TD. 4 can teach TD and ES but NOT EM. How many teachers can teach only TD?

sourceQuestion:

A. 44  B. 51  C. 79  D. 108

parsed.stem: The Venn diagram shows the preference of the student population for leisure activities. The diagram has three overlapping circles labeled 'Read Books', 'Play Sports', and 'Watch TV'. From the data given, the number of students who like to read books or play sports is:

sourceQuestion:
200 students are taking one or more courses out of Chemistry, Physics, and Mathematics. Registration records indicate course enrollment as follows: Chemistry (137), Physics (130), and Mathematics (85). Chemistry and Physics (50), Chemistry and Mathematics (37), and Physics and Mathematics (63). How many students are taking all 3 subjects?

A. 37  B. 43  C. 47  D. 53

parsed.stem: 200 students are taking one or more courses out of Chemistry, Physics, and Mathematics. Registration records indicate course enrollment as follows: Chemistry (137), Physics (130), and Mathematics (85). Chemistry and Physics (50), Chemistry and Mathematics (37), and Physics and Mathematics (63). How many students are taking all 3 subjects?

sourceQuestion:
Forty students watched films A, B and C over a week. Each student watched either only one film or all three. Thirteen students watched film A, sixteen students watched film B and nineteen students watched film C. How many students watched all three films?

A. 0
B. 2
C. 4
D. 8

parsed.stem: Forty students watched films A, B and C over a week. Each student watched either only one film or all three. Thirteen students watched film A, sixteen students watched film B and nineteen students watched film C. How many students watched all three films?

sourceQuestion:
In a sports academy of 300 people, 70 play only cricket, 15 play only hockey, 50 play only football, 25 play both cricket and hockey, 10 play both hockey and football and 25 play both cricket and football. The rest of them play all three sports. What is the percentage of people who play at least two sports?

A. 23.33%
B. 25.00%
C. 33.33%
D. 50.00%

parsed.stem: In a sports academy of 300 people, 70 play only cricket, 15 play only hockey, 50 play only football, 25 play both cricket and hockey, 10 play both hockey and football and 25 play both cricket and football. The rest of them play all three sports. What is the percentage of people who play at least two sports?

sourceQuestion:
A sphere of radius r is packed in a box of cubical shape. What should be the minimum volume (in cm^3) of the box that can enclose the sphere?

A. (4/3)*pi*r^3
B. pi*r^3
C. 8*r^3
D. 8*pi*r^3

parsed.stem: A sphere of radius r is packed in a box of cubical shape. What should be the minimum volume (in cm^3) of the box that can enclose the sphere?

sourceQuestion:
We have 2 rectangular sheets of paper, M and N, of dimensions 6 cm x 1 cm each. Sheet M is rolled to form an open cylinder by bringing the short edges of the sheet together. Sheet N is cut into equal square patches and assembled to form the largest possible closed cube. Assuming the ends of the cylinder are closed, the ratio of the volume of the cylinder to the volume of the cube is:

A. 1/2
B. 3/(2*pi)
C. 9/(pi)
D. 3/pi

parsed.stem: We have 2 rectangular sheets of paper, M and N, of dimensions 6 cm x 1 cm each. Sheet M is rolled to form an open cylinder by bringing the short edges of the sheet together. Sheet N is cut into equal square patches and assembled to form the largest possible closed cube. Assuming the ends of the cylinder are closed, the ratio of the volume of the cylinder to the volume of the cube is:

sourceQuestion:
Pipes P and Q can fill a storage tank in full with water in 10 and 6 minutes, respectively. Pipe R draws the water out from the storage tank at a rate of 34 litres per minute. P, Q and R operate at a constant rate. If it takes one hour to completely empty a full storage tank with all the pipes operating simultaneously, what is the capacity of the storage tank (in litres)?

A. 27.5
B. 60.0
C. 120.0
D. 127.5

parsed.stem: Pipes P and Q can fill a storage tank in full with water in 10 and 6 minutes, respectively. Pipe R draws the water out from the storage tank at a rate of 34 litres per minute. P, Q and R operate at a constant rate. If it takes one hour to completely empty a full storage tank with all the pipes operating simultaneously, what is the capacity of the storage tank (in litres)?

sourceQuestion:
5 skilled workers can build a wall in 20 days; 8 semi-skilled workers can build a wall in 25 days; 10 unskilled workers can build a wall in 30 days. If a team has 2 skilled, 6 semi-skilled and 5 unskilled workers, how long will the team take to build the wall?

A. 20 days
B. 18 days
C. 16 days
D. 15 days

parsed.stem: 5 skilled workers can build a wall in 20 days; 8 semi-skilled workers can build a wall in 25 days; 10 unskilled workers can build a wall in 30 days. If a team has 2 skilled, 6 semi-skilled and 5 unskilled workers, how long will the team take to build the wall?

sourceQuestion:
A transporter receives the same number of orders each day. Currently, he has some pending orders (backlog) to be shipped. If he uses 4 trucks, then at the end of the 4th day he can clear all the orders. Alternatively, if he uses only 3 trucks, then all the orders are cleared at the end of the 5th day. What is the minimum number of trucks required so that there will be no pending order at the end of the 3rd day?

A. 20
B. 5
C. 6
D. 7

parsed.stem: A transporter receives the same number of orders each day. Currently, he has some pending orders (backlog) to be shipped. If he uses 4 trucks, then at the end of the 4th day he can clear all the orders. Alternatively, if he uses only 3 trucks, then all the orders are cleared at the end of the 5th day. What is the minimum number of trucks required so that there will be no pending order at the end of the 3rd day?

sourceQuestion:

A. 16,500
B. 15,180
C. 11,000
D. 10,120

parsed.stem: The current erection cost of a structure is Rs. 13,200. If the labour wages per day increase by 1/5 of the current wages and the working hours decrease by 1/24 of the current period, then the new cost of erection in Rs. is

sourceQuestion:
IT was estimated that 42 men can complete a strip in a newly constructed highway connecting cities J and Q in 9 days. Due to an emergency, 14 men were sent to another project after 3 days of commencement of work. How many number of days, more than the original estimate, will be required to complete the strip?

A. 3 days
B. 5 days
C. 10 days
D. 13 days

parsed.stem: IT was estimated that 42 men can complete a strip in a newly constructed highway connecting cities J and Q in 9 days. Due to an emergency, 14 men were sent to another project after 3 days of commencement of work. How many number of days, more than the original estimate, will be required to complete the strip?

sourceQuestion:
Ananth takes 6 hours and Bharath takes 4 hours to read a book. Both started reading copies of the book at the same time. After how many hours is the number of pages to be read by Ananth, twice that to be read by Bharath? Assume Ananth and Bharath read all the pages with constant pace.

A. 1
B. 2
C. 3
D. 4

parsed.stem: Ananth takes 6 hours and Bharath takes 4 hours to read a book. Both started reading copies of the book at the same time. After how many hours is the number of pages to be read by Ananth, twice that to be read by Bharath? Assume Ananth and Bharath read all the pages with constant pace.

sourceQuestion:
P, Q, R and S are working on a project. Q can finish the task in 25 days, working alone for 12 hours a day. R can finish the task in 50 days, working alone for 6 hours per day. Q worked 12 hours a day but took sick leave in the beginning for two days. R worked 12 hours a day on all days. What is the ratio of work done by Q and R after 7 days from the start of the project?

A. 10 : 11
B. 11 : 10
C. 20 : 21
D. 21 : 20

parsed.stem: P, Q, R and S are working on a project. Q can finish the task in 25 days, working alone for 12 hours a day. R can finish the task in 50 days, working alone for 6 hours per day. Q worked 12 hours a day but took sick leave in the beginning for two days. R worked 12 hours a day on all days. What is the ratio of work done by Q and R after 7 days from the start of the project?

sourceQuestion:
S, M, E and F are working in shifts in a team to finish a project. M works with twice the efficiency of others but for half as many days as E worked. S and M have 12-hour shifts in a day, whereas E and F have 8-hour shifts. What is the ratio of contribution of M to contribution of E in the project?

A. 1 : 1
B. 1 : 2
C. 1 : 4
D. 2 : 1

parsed.stem: S, M, E and F are working in shifts in a team to finish a project. M works with twice the efficiency of others but for half as many days as E worked. S and M have 12-hour shifts in a day, whereas E and F have 8-hour shifts. What is the ratio of contribution of M to contribution of E in the project?

sourceQuestion:
Two machines M1 and M2 are able to execute any of four jobs P, Q, R, S. The machines can perform one job on one object at a time. Jobs P, Q, R, S take 30 minutes, 20 minutes, 60 minutes and 15 minutes each respectively. There are 10 objects each requiring exactly 1 job. Job Q is to be performed on 2 objects. Job R on 1 object, and Job S on 3 objects, Job P on 4 objects. What is the minimum time needed to complete all the jobs?

A. 2 hours  B. 2.5 hours  C. 3 hours  D. 3.5 hours

parsed.stem: Two machines M1 and M2 are able to execute any of four jobs P, Q, R, S. The machines can perform one job on one object at a time. Jobs P, Q, R, S take 30 minutes, 20 minutes, 60 minutes and 15 minutes each respectively. There are 10 objects each requiring exactly 1 job. Job Q is to be performed on 2 objects. Job R on 1 object, and Job S on 3 objects, Job P on 4 objects. What is the minimum time needed to complete all the jobs?

sourceQuestion:
1200 men and 500 women can build a bridge in 2 weeks. 900 men and 250 women will take 3 weeks to build the same bridge. How many men will be needed to build the bridge in one week?

A. 3000  B. 3300  C. 3600  D. 3900

parsed.stem: 1200 men and 500 women can build a bridge in 2 weeks. 900 men and 250 women will take 3 weeks to build the same bridge. How many men will be needed to build the bridge in one week?

sourceQuestion:
X bullocks and 3Y tractors take D days to plough a field. If we have half the number of bullocks and double the number of tractors, how many days will it take X bullocks alone to plough the field?

Note: From the given data X bullocks + 3 tractors take D days; X/2 bullocks + 6 tractors also take D days to plough the same field. Given answer corresponds to 30 days for bullocks alone.

A. 30  B. 35  C. 40  D. 45

parsed.stem:
X bullocks and 3Y tractors take D days to plough a field. If we have half the number of bullocks and double the number of tractors, how many days will it take X bullocks alone to plough the field?

Note: From the given data X bullocks + 3 tractors take D days; X/2 bullocks + 6 tractors also take D days to plough the same field. Given answer corresponds to 30 days for bullocks alone.
